The 6 Best Samsung Phones of 2026: Navigating the Choices to Find Your Ideal Device

Updated: March 2, 2026
|12 min read
From flips and foldables, to budget-friendly and top-of-the-line, there’s a Samsung phone for every taste, style and price point.

Jump to a Section

  1. Best Overall: Samsung Galaxy S26
  2. Best Camera: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra
  3. Best for Business: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold7
  4. Best Compact: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip7
  5. Best Value: Samsung Galaxy S25 FE
  6. Best Budget: Samsung Galaxy A36 5G

If you ask the experts, “What is the best Samsung phone out there?”, you’ll likely get a lot of different answers. That’s because finding the best phone depends on what’s important to you. Is it price? Battery life? Cameras? The latest tech? Or maybe it’s something else entirely.

We’ve compiled a list of the six best Samsung phones of 2026 based on their capabilities to help you find the perfect fit.

*This comparison guide was created by T-Mobile to help you explore Samsung smartphones in our store. The opinions expressed here reflect our editorial perspective based on publicly available features, specifications, and articles.  

Key takeaways 

  • Choose the Galaxy S26+ for the best balance of screen size, battery life (4,900mAh), and price, or the Galaxy S26 for similar performance in a more compact 6.3-inch design.
  • The Galaxy S26 Ultra is ideal for photography and productivity thanks to its quad rear camera system, S Pen, Privacy Display, Gorilla Glass Armor 2, and 60W wired charging.
  • Foldables like the Galaxy Z Fold7 and Z Flip7 offer multitasking flexibility and portability, while the Galaxy S25 FE and Galaxy A36 5G provide strong value at lower price points.

1. Best Overall: Samsung Galaxy S26+

With a larger 6.7-inch QHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X display, powerful Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor, and refined Galaxy AI tools, the Galaxy S26+ earns its spot as the most balanced phone in Samsung’s 2026 lineup. 

It delivers flagship-level performance without stepping into Ultra pricing—making it a strong choice for most people. 

Key Features 

  • 6.7" QHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X Display (2,600 nits peak brightness, 120Hz adaptive refresh rate)
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5
  • Android 16
  • Triple rear camera system (50MP Wide + 12MP Ultra-Wide + 10MP Telephoto)
  • 12MP Front Camera
  • 4,900mAh battery
  • 45W wired charging / 20W wireless charging
  • 256GB or 512GB storage
  • 12GB RAM
  • Corning® Gorilla® Glass Victus 2

What We Like Most 

The S26+ hits a sweet spot. You get strong battery life, smooth multitasking, and a sharp QHD+ display that stays bright even outdoors. 

Its camera system handles everyday photography well—with 8K video capture and improved image processing powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5. Galaxy AI features like Photo Assist, Now Brief, and Smart Suggestions help streamline daily tasks without overcomplicating the experience. 

Compared to the S25+, the S26+ benefits from faster processing, updated charging speeds, and Android 16 out of the box. 

Other Considerations 

Prefer something slightly smaller without giving up flagship power? The base S26 keeps the same processor and camera foundation as the S26+, making it a strong option for users who want performance in a slightly more pocket-friendly size. 

The S25+ is still a capable option at a lower price point if you don’t need the latest upgrades. 

2. Best Camera: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra

If photography is your priority, the Galaxy S26 Ultra stands at the top of Samsung’s lineup. 

With five total cameras and upgraded glass protection, it’s built for users who want more zoom, more detail, and more editing flexibility. 

Key Features 

  • 6.9" QHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X Display (2,600 nits, 120Hz adaptive refresh rate)
  • Corning® Gorilla® Glass Armor 2
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5
  • Android 16
  • Embedded S Pen
  • Rear Cameras:
    • 200MP Wide (f/1.4)
    • 50MP Ultra-Wide (f/1.9)
    • 50MP Telephoto (f/2.9)
    • 10MP Telephoto (f/2.4)
    • Optical zoom: 2x, 3x, 5x, 10x
    • Digital zoom up to 100x
  • 12MP Front Camera
  • 5,000mAh battery
  • 60W wired charging / 25W wireless charging
  • Storage up to 1TB

What We Like Most 

The 200MP main sensor delivers strong detail in bright conditions, while dual telephoto lenses provide flexible zoom options without relying entirely on digital crop. 

Night photography benefits from Samsung’s processing improvements, and 8K video capture remains available for creators who want higher resolution recording. 

The new Privacy Display feature allows you to reduce side-angle visibility when needed—helpful in public spaces. Combined with Gorilla Glass Armor 2, the Ultra also improves durability and glare resistance. 

Other Considerations 

The S26 Ultra is Samsung’s most expensive slab-style phone. If you don’t need 100x zoom, the S26+ offers similar performance at a lower cost. 

The S25 Ultra remains a capable alternative if found at a reduced price. 

3. Best for Business: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold7

Multitaskers who are using their smartphones as a tool for staying connected to the office on the go need their devices to do much more than offer talking and texting capabilities. The Galaxy Z Fold7 is a sleek, stylish tablet-phone hybrid that provides all the mobility of a phone with all the utility of a tablet. When closed, the device looks like a regular phone, with a nice 6.5-inch outer screen. Open the phone and it transforms into what amounts to be a massive 8-inch tablet, perfect for watching videos, hosting conference calls, checking social media, or reviewing documents on the go. Best of all, it’s the thinnest, sleekest Z Fold so far at just 4.3mm when unfolded and 8.9mm folded.

Key Features

  • External Screen: 6.5" Dynamic AMOLED 2X
  • Main Screen: 8" Dynamic AMOLED 2X
  • Flex Mode
  • Hands-free Video
  • Multi-window view
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite for Galaxy
  • Cameras: 10MP Front Facing Camera & 10MP Cover Camera when Folded, 200MP Wide, 10MP Telephoto, 12MP Ultra Wide
  • Galaxy AI
  • 4,400 mAh battery
  • Storage: 256GB / 512GB
  • RAM: 12GB

What We Like Most

The foldable screen means double the real estate for reviewing documents, video, and taking notes. Plus, it gives you access to the latest and greatest Galaxy AI features.

You can also navigate your Z Fold7 much like a PC, making quick work of viewing messages, email, and your favorite apps. Best of all, it’s so much slimmer and lightweight than its predecessors, even with its bigger-than-life functionality.

Other Considerations

The Galaxy Z Fold7 is one of the most expensive smartphones on the market. So, those who won’t be using its tablet functions on a day-to-day basis might not see much advantage to the foldable device over other Samsung flagship devices. Plus, the S Pen is now completely gone—one of the sacrifices that had to be made for a slimmer, lighter Z Fold7. When the phone unfolds, the hinge is now much stronger and tighter, and the crease is almost invisible compared to earlier models, but it’s still there.

4. Best Compact: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip7

For those who want all the connectivity of a smartphone with all the compact portability of a flip phone, the Galaxy Z Flip7 may be the perfect fit.

Fans of the Galaxy Z Flip7 love the massive edge-to-edge external display screen, and with the new thinner design (6.35 millimeters unfolded, 13.7 millimeters folded, and weighing just 188 grams) it’s the slimmest and sleekest Z Flip yet. When you’re ready to interact, the Z Flip7 transforms into a bigger-than-life smartphone, revealing a stunningly vivid display and access to all the latest Galaxy AI features.

Key Features

  • External Screen: 4.1" Super AMOLED
  • Main Screen: 6.9" FHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X Infinity Flex
  • Cameras: 50MP Wide, 10MP Front, 12MP Ultra Wide
  • 4,300mAh battery
  • Storage: 256GB / 512GB
  • RAM: 12GB

What We Like Most

The main benefit of the Z Flip7 is the fact that you can comfortably carry it in your purse or pocket without worrying about damaging the screen. Plus, the smaller, yet expansive outer screen lets you take care of tasks like checking your schedule, replying to messages, or using AI-powered tools without even unfolding the device.

If you liked the slim design of its predecessor, you’ll love the Z Flip7, which has the slimmest design yet, despite having its largest-ever cover screen.

Other Considerations

The crease, while definitely improved thanks to the upgraded hinge, is still visible and may get more noticeable with time. Cost may also be an issue for some at full price.

5. Best Value: Samsung Galaxy S25 FE

The Galaxy S25 FE (Fan Edition) is designed to deliver flagship-level essentials at a friendlier price. It keeps many of the premium Galaxy S25 features - like Galaxy AI, a pro-grade camera, and a vivid 6.7" display - while trimming away extras that power users may not need. It’s lightweight, durable, and smart, making it a strong value for anyone who wants performance and premium design without stretching their budget. 

Key Features

  • 6.7" FHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X Display with 120Hz refresh rate and slimmer bezels for an immersive experience 
  • Triple Camera System (50MP Wide + 12MP Ultra-Wide + 8MP Telephoto) + 12MP Selfie Camera with ProVisual Engine for improved portraits and low-light shots 
  • Exynos 2400 Processor with upgraded CPU/GPU/NPU and a larger vapor chamber for faster cooling 
  • Galaxy AI features including Circle to Search, Generative Edit, Instant Slow-mo, Photo Assist, and Gemini Live 
  • 4,900mAh Battery with 45W Super Fast Charging 2.0 
  • 8GB RAM with 128GB or 256GB storage options 
  • Durability: Gorilla Glass Victus+ and Armor Aluminum frame, IP68 dust and water resistance 

What We Like Most

The S25 FE stands out with its upgraded 12MP selfie camera powered by the ProVisual Engine, delivering clearer portraits and better low-light shots. Its triple-lens rear camera still packs a sharp 50MP main sensor with OIS for detailed photos and videos. 

Beyond the cameras, the 6.7-inch AMOLED display with slimmer bezels and a smooth 120Hz refresh rate makes scrolling and streaming feel premium. Combined with the larger 4,900mAh battery and 45W super fast charging, it’s built for all-day use with quick top-ups when you need them. 

Other Considerations

The S25 FE stands out with its upgraded 12MP selfie camera powered by the ProVisual Engine, delivering clearer portraits and better low-light shots. Its triple-lens rear camera still packs a sharp 50MP main sensor with OIS for detailed photos and videos. 

Beyond the cameras, the 6.7-inch AMOLED display with slimmer bezels and a smooth 120Hz refresh rate makes scrolling and streaming feel premium. Combined with the larger 4,900mAh battery and 45W super fast charging, it’s built for all-day use with quick top-ups when you need them. 

6. Best Budget: Samsung Galaxy A36 5G

The A36 5G is proof that a 5G phone doesn't have to cost a fortune. Its 5G performance and other great features, like the big, vivid screen, durable design, and solid camera system, make it a great buy.

Key Features

  • 6.7'' clear FHD+ Super AMOLED Display
  • Triple Camera Setup (50MP Main + 8MP Ultra Wide & 5MP Macro Cameras + 12MP Front Camera)
  • Qualcomm® SM6475 Processor
  • 5,000mAh battery
  • 128GB Storage
  • 6GB RAM
  • 6 years of OS & security updates

What We Like Most

The low price combined with 5G capabilities makes the A36 5G a big winner in our book. The 6.7" clear FHD+ Super AMOLED display is big, crisp, clear, and colorful. You also get a large 5,000mAh battery to keep you powered-up throughout the day. Plus, with six years of updates, you can keep your phone longer, making it a great value.

Other Considerations

For the money, there are many great aspects of the phone, but it’s still far from a flagship device. While the processor is an upgrade from the previous model, the phone still may not be as responsive as more expensive ones when it comes to demanding apps. You may also notice some lag when taking photos, playing games and such.

Now that you know more about the 6 best Samsung phones on the market right now, it’s time to consider all the pros and cons and find one that fits your lifestyle. Whether it’s a budget phone that packs a lot of punch for the price, a flagship phone with all the high-end features, or something in between, use this comprehensive guide to find the right Samsung phone for you.

FAQs for the best Samsung phones of 2026

What is the best Samsung phone in 2026? 

For most people, the Galaxy S26+ offers the best balance of performance, display size, battery life, and price. If camera performance and zoom are your top priorities, the Galaxy S26 Ultra is Samsung’s most advanced option. 

Which Samsung phone is best for photography? 

The Galaxy S26 Ultra is Samsung’s top camera phone in 2026, featuring: 
-200MP main camera 
-Dual telephoto lenses 
-Up to 100x digital zoom 
-8K video recording 
For everyday photography at a lower cost, the S26+ and S26 still provide strong 50MP main cameras. 

Which Samsung phone is best for business use? 

The Galaxy Z Fold7 is ideal for business users who want a larger internal display for multitasking, document review, and video calls. The Galaxy S26 Ultra is also strong for productivity thanks to the built-in S Pen. 

What is the most affordable Samsung phone in 2026? 

The Galaxy A36 5G is the most budget-friendly option on this list. It includes a 50MP main camera, 5,000mAh battery, and six years of OS and security updates.
